---
title: 'Growth without jobs: wage gains outpace employment recovery'
description: Faceted line chart showing both employment and wage changes by sector
  since the Great Recession.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-2-2b-line-chart-change-in-wage-sector-facet/
categories:
  - Chart
  - Labor Market
  - Economic Indicators
tags:
  - QCEW
  - Employment
  - Wages
  - Sectoral Analysis
  - Line Chart
image: 2.2B_line_chart_change_in_wage_sector_facet.svg
chart:
  title: 'Growth without jobs: wage gains outpace employment recovery'
  chartType: line
  interactive: false
  source: Bureau of Labor Statistics, U.S. Department of Labor
  sourceUrl: https://www.bls.gov/cew/
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - qcew-employment-wages
  - bls-cpi-deflators
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![This chart reveals the divergence between wage growth and employment recovery across sectors, highlighting that productivity gains have not translated to job creation.](./2.2B_line_chart_change_in_wage_sector_facet.svg)

## Overview

This chart reveals the divergence between wage growth and employment recovery across sectors, highlighting that productivity gains have not translated to job creation.

## Key Findings

- Real wages have increased substantially across all sectors and rurality
- Employment recovery lags significantly behind wage growth
- The wage-employment gap is most pronounced in rural tradable goods

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/sector_job_wage_changes_2B.R` in the producing project.


::: {.callout-note}
## Dangling references

The following slugs are referenced by this project but do not yet have nodes in Dataverse. They are intentionally preserved as future content needs:

- `dataset/bls-cpi-deflators`
:::
